title: Regex recall path materializes entire visits table into memory (OOM at scale)

findings.description: list_history_with_regex passes :pageLimit = -1 (unbounded) to LIST_HISTORY_SQL, collecting every visible visit row into a Vec, then post-filters with the regex in Rust. On a 14.4M-row archive this allocates ~4.3 GB of HistoryEntry objects before a single row is shown to the user. Even on the 264k-row dev archive this is already noticeable; at the target scale it will OOM or hang for many seconds.
findings.suggestedFix: Switch to a bounded candidate scan: stream rows in batches of 1000-5000, apply the regex filter per batch, stop once the page window is filled plus one lookahead. Add a response field like scannedRows so the frontend can show an honest 'scanned N rows, showing M matches -- narrow your filter' message in all three languages. This is already tracked in BACKLOG WORK-REVIEW-0612-FOLLOWUPS-A as 'History regex recall unbounded'.
